stance_by_source.summary: Shorted the triple top for continuation down toward ~58; for your spot account, accumulate physical silver lower toward ~58 — not the short.
Daily chart: price 65.40 falling, demand flipped to resistance for continuation down into the 57.5-58 accumulation band.
2H short from 67.77 (10x), triple-top 1-2-3 at ~71, stepping down 66.5 → swing low ~63-64 → macro demand 57.5-58.
Spot plan: accumulate around 58 after a closure, with deeper one-month breakout demand below.
The leveraged short is haram; your only angle is accumulating physical silver lower after the flush. Flags the 67.77 entry as 'a touch early' vs the plan.

conflicts: The extracted entry of $67.77 is the leveraged SHORT entry, not a level to buy at — and Zero himself called it 'a touch early' vs his triple-top plan; it is not an action for the spot account.

The leveraged silver short (CFD + 10x) is haram, so it is not investable; the only actionable read is buying physical silver lower, around 58, which appears on the chart but was missing from the extracted text.
plain_english: Every source points the same way — silver is falling. Zero is running a leveraged short on it, which is off-limits for you, and his plan is for the price to keep dropping toward roughly $58, where strong long-term buying has shown up before. The only thing here you can act on is buying physical silver, and the guidance is to wait and accumulate lower in that $58 area rather than at today's price. So hold off for now: let it fall into the zone before buying, with the option of a second, smaller buy near $50 if it drops further.
